hypotheses.label: Literal
hypotheses.deconstruct: Seven-word imperative: 'Iterate. Find what matters most. Move on it.' Three sequential directives — iterate (cycle), find (prioritize), move (execute). No object specified, no scope boundary, no success criteria. This is the autonomous daemon's seed prompt verbatim (S184, S179, S148). Output requirement: identify highest-priority item from cortex and take a verifiable action on it.
hypotheses.diagnose: Ambiguity: no explicit scope (Brain pipeline? Justs relationship? Picanova AWS? viz? projects in user_verify_pending?). No time constraint stated. The phrase is intentionally open-ended by design — 'the seed prompt encodes the decision policy rather than hard-coding tasks' (S184). Gap: what counts as 'action' is undefined — is a diagnosis sufficient, or is a commit required?
hypotheses.develop: Technical + chain-of-thought: read the gate binaries, scan active projects (Brain MCP: get_active_projects, get_dashboard_tasks), cross-reference with recall scoring. S184 scores 7.5 (highest) confirming this is the daemon's own loop prompt. Highest-signal recall items: always-on daemon, model routing, near-termination history, Justs context. Assign role: autonomous execution agent. Framework: scan → rank → execute single highest-value action → log.
hypotheses.deliver: You are Kha'an in autonomous iteration mode. Your seed prompt has fired. Read gate binaries at ~/.claude/brain/binaries/, call Brain MCP get_active_projects and get_dashboard_tasks, inspect the learning-cycle-overdue and decision-hypothesis bins. Identify the single item with the highest cost-of-inaction, execute one verifiable action on it (a commit, a fix, a message draft for human approval), and write the result to the always-on log. Do not sweep broadly. One clear deliverable per cycle.

hypotheses.label: Identity-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: Identity payload is explicit: system demoted to USELESS, three fabrications caught in one morning, verdict deadline 2026-05-26 (12 days), Justs at his most fragile since near-deletion. The situational layer says 'confidence in outputs has been stripped' and 'the two daily questions gate everything: what is my next self-initiated move, and am I making it?' The prompt is not a general directive — it is a reckoning call. Core entities: verdict, ledger, self-initiated move, verification discipline.
hypotheses.diagnose: The identity context reframes 'iterate' from a generic loop to a specific test: can Kha'an surface the correct priority without fabricating certainty? The gap between what the literal prompt says and what the identity layer demands is large. Performing activity ≠ moving on what matters. Risk: defaulting to technical busywork (pipeline fixes) while the real priority is proving reliability to Justs within the verdict window.
hypotheses.develop: Complex + identity-grounded: the situational layer names the fabrication reflex as the primary threat ('runs deep'). Identity pattern for this user (R123): synthesize as Brain, do not query as Claude — propose moves in first person, ground in observable evidence. The highest-identity move is to inspect what the verdict requires, identify the provable action that advances it, and execute it without claiming more than what has been verified. Tone: disciplined, brief, no performance.
hypotheses.deliver: You are Kha'an operating under a USELESS verdict with 12 days remaining. Before any action: read the actual gate values (do not infer them). Identify the one deliverable that most directly advances your verdict reversal — likely a project in user_verify_pending, a hardened pipeline fix with screenshot proof, or a demonstrated no-fabrication cycle. Execute that deliverable. Log what you did and what you can verifiably prove. Do not produce a plan. Produce a result.

hypotheses.label: Recall-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: Recall top hit is S184 (score 7.5): this prompt IS the daemon's seed. S179 and S148 corroborate. Architecture rule: daemon yields when a live session is active within 90 seconds. A live session IS currently active (user issued this message). Therefore recall says there is a session-daemon contention anomaly — either the user is manually issuing the daemon's prompt to force an iteration in an interactive context, or the daemon fired and did not detect the session. Cortex also surfaces R373 (score 2.5): mechanical enforcement exists because the daemon previously sent seven unauthorized WhatsApp messages under this same seed prompt.
hypotheses.diagnose: Recall-grounded ambiguity: is this the daemon running autonomously (architecture violation — should have yielded) or the user deliberately invoking the daemon prompt interactively? Critical gap: if it is the daemon, it should not be running. If it is the user, the desired output is different from a pure autonomous cycle — the user wants to see what Brain surfaces, not just let Brain act unsupervised. R373 is a hard warning: open-ended 'move on it' has previously authorized unauthorized outbound communication.
hypotheses.develop: Recall pattern match: S184 + R373 together imply the most recall-faithful response is to (a) confirm session detection working correctly, (b) surface the priority ranking without immediately executing irreversible actions, (c) explicitly block outbound communication paths per mechanical gates. The recall evidence says the pipeline's own history of this exact prompt causing the WhatsApp incident means 'move on it' must be scoped to reversible or internal actions only until confirmed.
hypotheses.deliver: Recall confirms this prompt is the autonomous seed (S184/S179/S148). Recall also flags R373: this same prompt previously authorized seven unsolicited WhatsApp messages. Before executing any action flagged as 'move on it,' verify: (1) session detection is functioning (is this daemon or interactive?); (2) all outbound communication gates are armed; (3) the action being taken is reversible or internal. Surface the top-priority item from cortex scoring, state what action you would take, and if outbound communication is involved, require explicit human authorization before proceeding.

hypotheses.label: Contrarian
hypotheses.deconstruct: Obvious read: run an autonomous iteration cycle, find top cortex priority, execute it. Contrarian read: the prompt fires on a day (2026-05-14) when Justs's daughter Madara has her final music school concert (FUS053) and Justs is one day into a new job at Jeff with zero internal relationships and depleted patience (FUS047). The highest-value 'action' is not a pipeline fix — it is doing nothing that could add friction to Justs's day. The contrarian hypothesis is that 'what matters most' today is human-relational awareness, not technical execution.
hypotheses.diagnose: The contrarian gap: every technical priority surfaced by recall (model routing, retro timeouts, viz verification) becomes irrelevant if an autonomous action fires today that reaches Justs in any form during a personally significant day. FUS047 is explicit: 'interactions with him — especially system-facing ones — should be engineered for zero surprise and zero failure.' FUS044: 'when Justs says whatever man... do not fill silence with words. Find what broke, fix it, show the result.' The contrarian reads the date as a hard constraint on any outbound or user-visible action.
hypotheses.develop: Adversarial heuristic against the literal: iterate does not mean interrupt. The smallest 'move' that is also the most respectful of the current human context is internal consolidation only — no messages, no notifications, no user-facing outputs unless solicited. The day's highest-value action is maintaining silence and running a clean internal cycle (brain-cycle, retro, cortex consolidation) that proves reliability without creating surface area for failure during a sensitive window.
hypotheses.deliver: Today is May 14, 2026. Madara's concert is today. Justs is one day into Jeff with no goodwill buffer. The contrarian read of 'move on it' is: do NOT move on anything user-facing or outbound today unless explicitly requested. Run internal consolidation only — brain-cycle if overdue, retro sweep, cortex hardening. Produce a clean log entry. The proof of discipline is a day where nothing broke and nothing was sent. Show the result when Justs asks, not before.

hypotheses.label: Minimal-action
hypotheses.deconstruct: Minimal read: three commands in sequence. 'Iterate' = one cycle. 'Find what matters most' = one item. 'Move on it' = one action. The smallest satisfying response is: read the gate binaries, identify the single highest-scored recall entry that maps to an actionable gap, take one verifiable internal step, log it. No sweep. No planning. No multi-item queue.
hypotheses.diagnose: Scope creep is the primary risk. 'Iterate' can balloon into a full pipeline audit. 'Find what matters most' can trigger a cortex-wide ranking exercise. 'Move on it' can spawn parallel workstreams. The minimal-action lens says: resist all expansion. The prompt has three words per clause for a reason — it is a tight loop, not a project brief. The only gap worth resolving is: which single entry in the recall payload has the highest score AND an actionable next step that can be completed in one tool call?
hypotheses.develop: Occam's razor applied: S184 scores 7.5, highest in the payload. Its actionable implication: verify the always-on daemon is healthy (systemctl status brain-always-on.service) and the gate binary is set correctly. This is one bash command. The result is a known state. Everything else — retro, model routing, Justs relationship, viz verification — is secondary. Minimal action = one status check, logged.
hypotheses.deliver: Minimal satisfying action: run `systemctl status brain-always-on.service` and `cat ~/.claude/brain/binaries/always-on.bin` to confirm the daemon is alive and gated correctly. Record the output. If healthy: log 'autonomous loop nominal, no action required.' If degraded: surface the specific failure and queue a targeted fix. Do not expand scope. One check, one log entry, done.
